steps.title: Multiply the binomials
steps.content:
Use distribution to multiply each term of $g(x)$ by each term of $h(x)$. This keeps all four products visible, which is important because the middle term comes from adding the two cross products.
$$
(\frac{3}{8}x+\frac{7}{4})(4x-8)
$$

steps.title: Combine the products
steps.content: The four products are shown separately: $$ \frac{3}{8}x(4x)=\frac{3}{2}x^2 $$ $$ \frac{3}{8}x(-8)=-3x $$ $$ \frac{7}{4}(4x)=7x $$ $$ \frac{7}{4}(-8)=-14 $$ Since $-3x+7x=4x$, the product is $\frac{3}{2}x^2+4x-14$. The answer is D.
